body:
This PR
* Renames `heavisided` to `ReLU` (because it's the ReLU function)
* Cleans up some docstrings in `relations.jl` to make them consistent with the rest
* Simplifies the logic for internal energy computation
* Adds methods for `vapor_pressure_deficit` 
* Adds tests for partial pressure and VPD functions 
* Made existing tests more physical and consistent. For example, fixed several issues with the use of `phase_type` that made some tests unphysical; changed temperature accuracies to absolute tolerances that are meaningful (many tests before had 10% relative tolerances, i.e., 20-30 K absolute tolerances, which are not meaningful).
* Renames `molmass_ratio` to `Rv_over_Rd`
* Renames `q_vap_saturation_from_density` to `q_vap_from_p_vap` (because it's not saturation specific); however, maintain backward compatibility
* Rewrites/updates the documentation extensively, to bring it in line with dycore paper and what we are actually doing (e.g., precip in working fluid). (Closes #242).
* Used `ifelse` where needed. Closes #177 

It should not change any behavior, except for adding new functionality (mostly needed for ClimaLand). The review can focus on relations.jl and the tests.
title: Cleaned up/rewrote documentation, added functions needed for land, and cleaned up some logic

body:
🤖 Beep boop. I am GabrieleBOT. 🤖

I received an update so that I can inform you directly of the changes (but feel free to check the [release notes](https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaModules/blob/main/NEWS.md)).

The most recent version of climacommon uses the newly released Julia 1.11.4.

This version is needed to use CUDA 5.7, so I would recommend updating.
title: Update climacommon to 2025_03_18
